meta.description: Sample French vocabulary dataset for the French Learning App

example_sentences.fr: Bonjour, comment allez-vous ?
example_sentences.en: Hello, how are you?

example_sentences.fr: Il dit bonjour à ses collègues.
example_sentences.en: He says good morning to his colleagues.

vocabulary.usage_notes: Used until roughly midday; after that use 'bonsoir'. Never use with close friends informally — 'salut' is more natural.

example_sentences.fr: Merci beaucoup pour votre aide.
example_sentences.en: Thank you very much for your help.

example_sentences.fr: — Voulez-vous du café ? — Non, merci.
example_sentences.en: — Would you like coffee? — No, thank you.

vocabulary.usage_notes: Merci alone = yes thank you; 'Non, merci' = no thank you.
vocabulary.memory_tip: Sounds like 'mercy' — show mercy with gratitude

example_sentences.fr: Ma maison est grande.
example_sentences.en: My house is big.

example_sentences.fr: Je rentre à la maison.
example_sentences.en: I'm going home.

vocabulary.usage_notes: À la maison = at home. La maison vs. le chez-soi (more personal/emotional sense of home).
vocabulary.memory_tip: Mansion comes from maison — both mean dwelling

example_sentences.fr: Je mange une pomme.
example_sentences.en: I eat an apple.

example_sentences.fr: Qu'est-ce que tu veux manger ce soir ?
example_sentences.en: What do you want to eat tonight?

vocabulary.usage_notes: Spelling change: nous mangeons (e retained before -ons to keep soft g sound).

example_sentences.fr: Un verre d'eau, s'il vous plaît.
example_sentences.en: A glass of water, please.

example_sentences.fr: L'eau de cette rivière est froide.
example_sentences.en: The water in this river is cold.

vocabulary.usage_notes: Plural 'eaux' is silent — same sound /o/. Common compounds: eau-de-vie, eau minérale.
vocabulary.memory_tip: Eau = O (the letter), just the sound 'oh'

example_sentences.fr: Elle travaille dans un hôpital.
example_sentences.en: She works in a hospital.

example_sentences.fr: Je travaille de 9h à 17h.
example_sentences.en: I work from 9am to 5pm.

vocabulary.usage_notes: The -ill- is pronounced /j/ not /l/. Cognate with 'travail' (hardship in medieval Latin).
vocabulary.memory_tip: Travel → travail → travailler: work is like travelling through the day

example_sentences.fr: C'est un beau paysage.
example_sentences.en: It's a beautiful landscape.

example_sentences.fr: Elle est très belle.
example_sentences.en: She is very beautiful.

vocabulary.usage_notes: beau (m sg), belle (f sg), beaux (m pl), belles (f pl). Before masculine vowel: bel (un bel homme).
vocabulary.memory_tip: Beauty = belle (Sleeping Beauty = La Belle au bois dormant)

example_sentences.fr: Je voudrais un café, s'il vous plaît.
example_sentences.en: I would like a coffee, please.

example_sentences.fr: Qu'est-ce que tu veux faire ?
example_sentences.en: What do you want to do?

vocabulary.usage_notes: Use conditional 'voudrais' for polite requests. 'Je veux' sounds demanding.
vocabulary.memory_tip: Will = vouloir: I will(want) to go

example_sentences.fr: J'habite dans un quartier calme.
example_sentences.en: I live in a quiet neighbourhood.

example_sentences.fr: Le Quartier Latin est célèbre pour ses librairies.
example_sentences.en: The Latin Quarter is famous for its bookshops.

vocabulary.usage_notes: Un quart = a quarter. Quartier is literally a 'quarter' of a city.
vocabulary.memory_tip: Quarter → quartier: neighbourhood is one quarter of the city

example_sentences.fr: Il est intelligent ; cependant, il manque d'expérience.
example_sentences.en: He is intelligent; however, he lacks experience.

example_sentences.fr: La solution est simple. Cependant, peu de gens la connaissent.
example_sentences.en: The solution is simple. However, few people know it.

vocabulary.usage_notes: More formal than 'mais'. Placed at beginning of clause or after semicolon. Synonyms: toutefois, néanmoins, pourtant.
vocabulary.memory_tip: Ce + pendant (during/while) → while this is true, however…

example_sentences.fr: Le gouvernement accorde des subventions aux agriculteurs.
example_sentences.en: The government grants subsidies to farmers.

example_sentences.fr: Cette association reçoit une subvention de la mairie.
example_sentences.en: This association receives a grant from the town hall.

vocabulary.usage_notes: Cognate with English 'subvention'. Colloquial shortening: 'subvention' itself is already formal.
vocabulary.memory_tip: Sub + venir (to come) → money that comes under/underneath to support

example_sentences.fr: L'épanouissement personnel est un objectif de vie pour beaucoup.
example_sentences.en: Personal fulfilment is a life goal for many.

example_sentences.fr: Ce travail lui permet un réel épanouissement professionnel.
example_sentences.en: This job allows him true professional fulfilment.

vocabulary.usage_notes: From épanouir (to blossom). The verb form: s'épanouir. Widely used in psychology and self-help contexts.
vocabulary.memory_tip: épanouir = to blossom (like a flower opening) → épanouissement = the blossoming itself

example_sentences.fr: Nonobstant les difficultés, le projet a abouti.
example_sentences.en: Notwithstanding the difficulties, the project succeeded.

example_sentences.fr: Cette clause s'applique nonobstant toute disposition contraire.
example_sentences.en: This clause applies notwithstanding any contrary provision.

vocabulary.usage_notes: Exclusively formal/legal register. Synonymous with 'malgré' (informal), 'en dépit de' (semi-formal). Commonly found in contracts and legislation.
vocabulary.memory_tip: Non + obstant (from obstare, to stand against) → not standing in the way of

example_sentences.fr: Je me souviens de mon premier jour d'école.
example_sentences.en: I remember my first day of school.

example_sentences.fr: Tu te souviens de son nom ?
example_sentences.en: Do you remember his name?

vocabulary.usage_notes: Always reflexive. Takes de: se souvenir de qqch/qqn. Contrast with 'se rappeler' + direct object (se rappeler son nom).
vocabulary.memory_tip: souvenir = a keepsake that makes you remember → se souvenir = to remember

example_sentences.fr: Il n'a pas étudié et pourtant il a réussi.
example_sentences.en: He didn't study and yet he passed.

example_sentences.fr: C'est pourtant simple !
example_sentences.en: And yet it's simple!

vocabulary.usage_notes: Can appear mid-sentence or at the start. More emphatic than 'cependant'. Often expresses surprise or contradiction.
vocabulary.memory_tip: Pour (for) + tant (so much) → for all that = yet/nonetheless
